transcript.clean_text: hello and welcome to highlight some stage 8 in the 2019 Tour de France on GC and racing a quick recap of yesterday before we launch into the action and TJ Van Garderen key team member of the EF squad crashed early in the stage he finished but x-rays have revealed the frats to his hand and so he didn't take the start today the finale was a classic sprint finish it was the Dutchman Dylan Bruno vagin who just edged out Caleb you take his first win of this year's tour Julia ciccone held on to his slim leading overall classification just six seconds up on Julian and a Philippe who it must be said will probably have been licking his lips at what was in store today a very hilly 200 kilometers from Macomb to Santa Tien no less than five second category climbs and to a third category ascents a total of close to 4,000 meters of climbing on a stage like today we'd expect a big fight to get into the breakaway as the chances of little lasting to the finish a better than usual but the first move was the one that stuck with Thomas de Ghent Niki Terpstra and Ben King poor man Schmidt as you can see here it was just behind but agonizingly couldn't latch onto the wheels of the escaping riders impressively though Alejandra De Marchi had a go a few kilometers later and did successfully bridge across although it took 15 KS full gas on his own to do so the intermediate sprint came early in the stage and terpstra took the points in the breakaway for what it was worth but back in the bunch Bahrain mridu put in a full lead out for their man Sonny Cole Pirelli as you can see 65 KS an hour here but in the end it's Elia Viviani followed by Sagan and Matthews and not Cole Bradley who take it the gap to the leaders was held pretty tightly by the peloton and around the three-minute to 4 minute mark it's clear from which teams are setting the pace as to which riders were feeling confident Marcus Burkhardt running for Bora hansgrohe working for the interests of Peters again and Chad hard o son Webb for Michael Matthews now both of those riders are probably the best climbers of all the sprinters so they were trying to ensure both the breakaway stayed in check and also send as many of the other sprinters as possible out of the bag now back up front and Thomas again was clearly a man on a mission he crested every climb of the day first and then both he and de Marquis accelerated dispatching their two companions and forging on as a pair clearly the stronger riders having just two fully committed riders would give them the best chances of holding off the peloton Astana started to ride on the cook de Vere's with 63 kilometers to go as you can see the gap is three minutes and 48 now up front and our two leaders were not hanging around both of them nearly coming to grief on this corner de Marquis just laying it down they're in a very controlled fashion but he regained contact with de Ghent soon after Peters again here in trouble at the back of what is now a fast charging peloton under the impetus of Astana and EF with 15 clumps to go and the Bunch gets caught out on a very tricky right-hander we can see that the whole of team in us getting caught out a quick bite change and a very well drilled chase is what's in store you can see from the action replay that it's actually an EF rider that runs wide and then who looks like Michael Woods grabbing a handful of front brake hitting the deck and taking out any AHS and that looks like a big big impact that would do that to a bi Garant ominous pulls all his team back by again burn out to help him get back to the punch meanwhile at front Tom's de Ghent is now forged on ahead dropping de Marquis and pressing that final king of the mountains alone just 34 seconds ahead of the peloton back there you can see a la Philippe and thibaut pinot attacking hard over the top of that final third cattle climb there's actually a time bonus up for grabs there as well so a low poly takes a valuable five seconds to close that gap up to giulio Ciccone in the yellow jersey competition miraculously they're at Thomas again is still out front and holds on to his 32nd advantage despite the it's an alle Philippe and thibaut pinot on the steep descent into Sante Tian and almost unbelievably to Ghent holds on to take stage victory thibaut pinot crosses the finish line in second place a consolation for an awfully but that he will go into tomorrow stage Bastille Day in France wearing that all-important yellow jersey you can see the poets on finishing in 26 seconds later still some fast riders in that bunch with Matthews again and Trenton both the any our team leaders plan out and Garin Thomas also finishing is safely close to the front but the celebrations from Thomas de Ghent well well-deserved slim six second margin of victory not bad effort after 200 kilometers out front but this man ala Philippe as I said will be starting at the Bastille Day stage tomorrow back in the yellow jersey now 23 seconds ahead of Giulio Ciccone 53 seconds up on thibaut pinot who will be pretty pleased to have got an extra 20 second buffer over some of the other big hitters like gallant thomas and a Gamper now now tomorrow is almost as relentless is today albeit slightly shorter 170 kilometers starting in Santi TN and finishing in Roman bar days hometown of brood fewer categorized climbs and today put a first cap after just 36 kilometers and some very tough racing roads right through to the finish could that be the perfect launch pad with just 12 kilometers to go join our friends at your sport to watch it live or subscribe to GCN racing and hit that Bell icon so you can watch our highlights as soon as they go live just after the stage
